hero.question: US x Iran ceasefire extended by April 21, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The market is pricing a 65% chance of a US-Iran ceasefire extension by April 2026, which assumes either dramatic de-escalation or negotiation success despite zero credible diplomatic signals in the past 90 days. The -9.5 point drop suggests smart money is finally pricing in what should be obvious: geopolitical momentum is running the opposite direction, and bettors were anchored too high on old optimism.

daily_take.headline: Iran Ceasefire Odds Crater 9.5pts. The Market Just Priced In Reality.
daily_take.deck: The US-Iran ceasefire extension dropped from 74% to 65% in 24 hours on fresh tensions and intelligence reports suggesting neither side trusts the current framework. At 65%, you're still getting paid 0.54:1 to bet on an unstable equilibrium holding through April. Smart money is fading this. The real action is the permanent peace deal at 15.5%, down 15pts, which is now severely mispriced relative to a ceasefire renewal. If talks collapse, both fall. If they hold, the permanent deal rallies hard.

sidebar.headline: Permanent peace deal got absolutely hammered (down 15pts) but still seems disconnected from ceasefire logic. If ceasefire holds at 65%, permanent deal should trade higher than 15.5%. Arbitrage play here.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Permanent Peace: 15.5%

sidebar.headline: Flyweight prelim moving +5pts on late fighter news. Low volume at $107K suggests sharp money hasn't loaded up yet. Check weigh-ins before the bell.
sidebar.label: Filho vs. Rocha: 56.0%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic normalization collapsed 20pts in one day, now at 20.5%. This is the tell. Market priced in escalation risk hard. If tensions cool, this reprices fast.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal Traffic: 20.5%
